description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, constructed from dark, weathered wooden staves held together by four prominent black metal hoops. The top of the barrel is closed with a wooden lid, which features a small, raised bung or plug near the edge, likely for filling or emptying contents. The wood surface shows a natural grain pattern and some signs of wear, giving it an aged, rustic appearance. The metal bands are slightly reflective and tightly fitted, providing structural reinforcement. There are no visible handles or decorative markings on the exterior. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage areas, or near workshops, used for storing liquids or dry goods.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with black metal hoops and a closed wooden lid featuring a small plug. The surface is rustic and aged, with no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full circular lid with a small plug and all four metal hoops clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wood grain and the plug on the lid, with the metal hoops wrapping around the body.
description_view_2: Rear view with the lid and plug visible, showing the curvature of the barrel and the continuous metal bands.
description_view_3: Angled view emphasizing the plug on the lid and the alignment of the metal hoops around the barrel.
